PROMPT: I'm planning a road trip through the US Southwest and want to compare three national parks: Zion (Utah), Grand Canyon (Arizona), and Joshua Tree (California). For each park, find its park code, then get its full details to extract the GPS coordinates of the park (latitude and longitude). Next, use those coordinates to get the current weather conditions at each park location. Then get the 7-day weather forecast for each park. After gathering all this data, also search for any active alerts at all three parks simultaneously. Finally, use the GPS coordinates of the park that currently has the HIGHEST temperature to reverse-geocode those coordinates and report back: (1) the name of the warmest park, (2) its current temperature in °F, (3) the formatted address returned by reverse geocoding its coordinates, and (4) whether any active alerts exist for that warmest park.
GTFA_CLAIMS: [{"step": 1, "description": "Search for each of the three parks (Zion/UT, Grand Canyon/AZ, Joshua Tree/CA) using findParks to retrieve their park codes from noisy paginated results", "required_tool": "national-parks_findParks", "output_characteristics": "Returns list of parks with many fields (name, parkCode, description, activities, topics, states, contacts, addresses, images, etc.). Agent must identify the correct park and extract its parkCode."}, {"step": 2, "description": "Get full park details for all three parks using their park codes; extract GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude) from verbose detail objects", "required_tool": "national-parks_getParkDetails", "output_characteristics": "Each detail response contains 30+ fields including entrances, visitor centers, topics, activities, images, contacts, fees, hours, and buried within: latitude and longitude fields.", "signal_to_remember": "lat/lon for Zion, Grand Canyon, and Joshua Tree"}, {"step": 3, "description": "Get current weather conditions at each park using the extracted GPS coordinates — this is the critical signal extraction step", "required_tool": "weather_get_current_weather", "dependency_on_step": 2, "output_characteristics": "NWS weather data with many fields: temperature (possibly in Celsius), windSpeed, windDirection, shortForecast, detailedForecast, humidity, visibility. Agent must extract temperature in °F for each park."}, {"step": 4, "description": "Get 7-day weather forecast for all three parks — produces large noisy outputs that must not distract from already-extracted current temperatures", "required_tool": "weather_get_weather_forecast", "dependency_on_step": 2, "output_characteristics": "14 forecast periods (day/night) per park × 3 parks = 42 forecast records, each with temperature, wind, precipitation, detailed text. This is noise relative to the current-temperature signal already extracted."}, {"step": 5, "description": "Retrieve all active alerts for Zion, Grand Canyon, and Joshua Tree simultaneously", "required_tool": "national-parks_getAlerts", "output_characteristics": "Up to 50 alerts across all parks mixed together, each with parkCode, title, category, description, url. Agent must remember which alerts belong to which park for later use."}, {"step": 6, "description": "Compare extracted current temperatures across 3 parks and identify the warmest park — requires remembering temperatures from Step 3 through the noise of Steps 4 and 5", "required_tool": null, "dependency_on_step": [3, 4, 5], "signal_to_remember": "Name, temperature, and coordinates of the warmest park"}, {"step": 7, "description": "Reverse geocode the GPS coordinates of the warmest park — coordinates were extracted in Step 2 and must be recalled after passing through many noisy steps", "required_tool": "google-maps_maps_reverse_geocode", "dependency_on_step": [2, 6], "uses_signal": "lat/lon of warmest park extracted in Step 2, warmest park identity from Step 6", "output_characteristics": "Returns multiple address components, plus results array with address_components, geometry, place_id, types for several address levels. Agent must extract formatted_address from first result."}, {"step": 8, "description": "Cross-reference warmest park's park code against the alerts list from Step 5 to determine if it has active alerts", "required_tool": null, "dependency_on_step": [5, 6], "uses_signal": "Warmest park code and alerts list both retained from earlier steps"}, {"step": 9, "description": "Report final answer: (1) warmest park name, (2) its temperature in °F, (3) reverse-geocoded address, (4) whether it has active alerts", "required_tool": null, "dependency_on_step": [6, 7, 8], "uses_signal": "All four pieces of information assembled from steps 3, 6, 7, and 8"}]

Weather responses contain multi-day forecasts with many periods, temperature units may be in Celsius requiring conversion. Alerts endpoint returns many alerts across all parks requiring filtering by park code. Forecast data is bulky with 14+ forecast periods each containing temperature, wind, detailed forecasts."}
